title: einadia polygonoides
intro: Einadia polygonoides, also known as creeping thyme or creeping pennyroyal, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is a low-growing, spreading groundcover native to Australia, with small leaves and tiny white or pink flowers. The leaves of Einadia polygonoides have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and relieve pain. Einadia polygonoides can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used to treat various health issues, including indigestion, bloating, and menstrual cramps.
